Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the provided code the anomalies are a Loop between Evaluation and Approval b XOR that may skip customer notification c Partial ordering that might enable premature closing d Possible out-of-sequence execution

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop operator means that the process can repeatedly evaluate and approve the same claim The XOR operator allows for the possibility of skipping the customer notification step The partial order includes an edge from A to C so C can occur after A without waiting for the loop and XOR

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 1 The business process might require multiple rounds of evaluation and approval for highvalue claims Hypothesis 2 The process might have been intended to allow for reevaluation if the approval is denied Hypothesis 1 There might be certain types of claims where notification is not required Hypothesis 2 The notification step might have been optional due to a technical issue Hypothesis 1 The process might have been designed to allow claims to be closed without evaluation Hypothesis 2 The modeler might have intended to have a parallel structure but accidentally added an edge from A to C

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for multiple evaluations and approvals on the same claim with query SELECT claim_id activity COUNT as count FROM claim_events WHERE activity IN E P GROUP BY claim_id activity HAVING COUNT 1 Check for claims that were closed without an evaluation or approval Check for claims that were closed without notification Check for claims that were assigned and then closed without any other events
